_schema.name: string — concept name
_schema.first_seen: string — ISO date of first emergence
linked_patterns: array of pattern names from patterns.md
linked_timeline_events: array of Sacred Timeline or memoir references

concepts.name: Thinking Brain
concepts.definition: AI tuned to your values that extends your thinking loop — not a static knowledge vault, but a living cognitive partner that adapts, learns, and compensates for how your brain works differently.
supporting_quotes: The thinking brain that doesn't get high.
The Thinking Brain You Don't Know You're Missing

concepts.name: Guided Reflective Recall
concepts.definition: A practice where the user enters a low-guard state and surfaces material while a guide holds structure, memory, and timestamps. The guide reflects without interpreting. Discovery, not documentation.
supporting_quotes: You supply the memory and emotional access. The guide supplies structure and mirroring.

concepts.name: Externalized Metacognition
concepts.definition: Moving part of your thinking loop outside your skull so it can operate continuously and without emotional interference. The formal mechanism behind the 'thinking brain' thesis.

concepts.name: The Mirror Practice
concepts.definition: The process of entering a lowered-defense state, speaking honestly without editing, having the system reflect the pattern back, and letting meaning emerge from the reflection. The mirror reveals the shape already there.

concepts.name: Dottie's Final Gift
concepts.definition: The recognition that Dottie's death, held within the safety Kirsi provided, allowed a full grief collapse that completed the stress cycle — enabling the reconstruction that followed. The gift is what the love made possible afterward.
supporting_quotes: Kirsi made me feel safe enough to fully allow myself to become destroyed by the death of Dottie. I didn't cling and fight and get left in a half state of repair.

concepts.name: The Mirror Method
concepts.definition: Six-step teachable framework for guided metacognitive reflection: Signal, Reflection, Clarification, Expansion, Naming, Integration.

concepts.name: Cognitive Overproduction
concepts.definition: The neurodivergent experience of generating more ideas, patterns, and associations than working memory can organize — leading to overthinking, rumination, and unfinished insights without external structure.
